Everything you need to stay compliant

CraneCheck replaces your paper inspection forms with a digital system that's faster, more reliable, and always audit-ready.

Daily Pre-Shift Inspections

29 CFR 1926.1412 compliant checklists for mobile, tower, overhead, and crawler cranes. Tap through each checkpoint in under 3 minutes.

Equipment Registry

Track your entire fleet in one place. Crane type, serial number, certifications, and complete inspection history for every unit.

Deficiency Tracking

Critical failures auto-lock cranes from operation. Flag issues, assign corrective actions, and track resolution with photo evidence.

PDF Audit Reports

One-click OSHA-ready documentation. Inspector name, date, equipment ID, findings, and photos — all in a professional PDF.

Team Management

NCCCO cert tracking, role-based access. Safety directors see everything, operators see their assigned equipment.

Mobile-First

Works on any phone, even with spotty cell service. Offline mode syncs when you're back in range. Built for gloves and bright sun.

Built on OSHA regulations,
not around them.

Every CraneCheck template maps directly to OSHA's crane inspection standards. Your operators follow the checklist — compliance happens automatically.

The cost of non-compliance

OSHA fines up to $165,514 per willful violation. After a crane accident, investigators subpoena inspection records first. Missing records = negligence liability.

1926.1412(d)

Each Shift Inspection

Visual inspection of 13+ items before every shift — controls, wire rope, hydraulics, safety devices.

1926.1412(e)

Monthly Inspection

Thorough inspection by qualified person. Must document date, inspector, equipment ID, and findings.

1926.1412(f)

Annual Inspection

Comprehensive inspection with certification record retained until next annual + 2 months.

1910.179

Overhead & Gantry

Daily visual + monthly documented + annual comprehensive for all overhead and gantry cranes.

3 minutes. Every shift. Done.

Faster than filling out a paper form — and infinitely more useful.

01

Open CraneCheck

Select your crane from the equipment registry. The correct OSHA checklist loads automatically based on crane type.

02

Walk the Inspection

Tap through each checkpoint — controls, wire rope, hydraulics, safety devices. Snap photos of any issues. Under 3 minutes.

03

Submit & Done

Your signed inspection record is stored, timestamped, and available for any audit. Generate PDF reports anytime.

What happens when OSHA shows up and your inspection logs are missing?

Every year, crane companies face devastating fines because they can't produce inspection records. Don't be one of them.

$165,514
per willful violation

OSHA's maximum penalty for willful violations. A single missed inspection on a single crane can trigger this.

Immediate
equipment shutdown

OSHA can issue an imminent danger order, shutting down your crane operations on the spot. Your project stops cold.

Criminal
liability

Knowing violations that result in worker death can lead to criminal prosecution. Paper logs that "went missing" won't save you.

Trusted by crane companies nationwide

See why safety directors are switching from paper to CraneCheck.

“We had OSHA show up last month. Pulled up every inspection on my phone in 30 seconds. The inspector said it was the cleanest documentation he'd ever seen. CraneCheck paid for itself that one visit.”

Tony Marchetti

Marchetti Crane Services · Houston, TX

“My guys used to 'forget' their paper checklists half the time. Now they tap through it on their phone before their first lift. I can see every inspection from my desk. No more chasing paperwork.”

Dave Kowalski

Midwest Steel Erectors · Chicago, IL

“We manage 35 cranes across 8 jobsites. Before CraneCheck, tracking inspections was a nightmare of binders and spreadsheets. Now everything's digital, searchable, and audit-ready. Game changer.”

Linda Chen

Pacific Crane Rental · Seattle, WA

Simple pricing. Cheaper than one OSHA fine.
